text:
Once upon a time, in a quiet town nestled between rolling hills and gentle streams, there lived a curious hedgehog named Hugo. Hugo was not just any hedgehog; he was known throughout the land for his insatiable curiosity and his remarkable ability to learn. His most prized possession wasn't his spines or his quick escapes—it was a small, leather-bound dictionary.

Every morning, after nibbling on a freshly gathered handful of blueberries, Hugo would scramble back to his cozy burrow. There he'd sprawl out, his eyes wide open, ready to absorb knowledge.

One peculiar afternoon, while rummaging through his collection of finds, Hugo discovered a dusty old letter—a proclamation from the town's historical society announcing an annual reading competition. This was unheard of! Books were treasures, not prizes.

Driven by a voracious appetite for adventure, Hugo figured this couldn't just be another normal day. He memorized every detail from the letter. The competition would feature readings from obscure classics
